effort_complexity.rationale: Known shape (bash precedent + cli.go), breadth across two roles + env hygiene + error taxonomy + fake-binary harness.

design_decisions.choice: internal/driver ErrKind vocabulary (config/transient/auth/provider/protocol, package-scoped string constants since internal/driver cannot import internal/model); non-zero CLI exit maps to ErrKindAuth (not a generic provider label), matching internal/model/cli.go's existing coarse non-zero-exit-is-auth heuristic exactly
options: Blunt: non-zero exit -> auth, matching cli.go's existing coarse heuristic exactly (chosen)
Precise: stderr pattern-match for login-failure text, non-matches fall back to a generic provider label
Leave non-zero exit -> generic provider label (original design proposal) and defer the terminal-halt gap to S06/S07
design_decisions.human_decision: Brad, 2026-07-03, Captain design review for S02 (pin 2 + follow-up technical fork). Chose Option 1 (extend the vocabulary now) over deferring to S06/S07 or accepting the regression; on the follow-up fork, chose the blunt non-zero-exit-is-auth heuristic over stderr pattern-matching.
design_decisions.rationale: internal/run/slice.go:487's model.IsTerminal/KindAuth fail-fast is a live, working safety property that S07-scheduler-failfast's own AC-03 already assumes survives the driver rewire. Matching cli.go's existing, already-production-validated non-zero-exit-is-auth heuristic preserves that property without inventing a new, unvalidated stderr-pattern-matching mechanism that could silently miss a real auth failure on CLI wording drift. Accepted cost: a genuine non-auth CLI crash is mislabeled auth — the same imprecision cli.go already ships today, not a new regression.
